political.s: Aveda is owned by Estée Lauder Companies, a publicly-traded parent company. Aveda itself does not conduct direct political advocacy. Member of climate-focused organizations including BICEP coalition supporting EPA carbon pollution standards.

charity.s: Aveda has raised over $72 million since 1999 through Earth Month campaigns for clean water initiatives, primarily partnering with charity: water. Supports breast cancer research through annual October campaigns. Provides support to local communities and nonprofits globally.
charity.focus: Clean water access, breast cancer research, environmental conservation

environment.s: Pioneer in environmental responsibility; first beauty company to sign Ceres Principles (1989) and achieve Cradle to Cradle certification. Operates on 100% wind and solar energy, uses 100% post-consumer recycled packaging, maintains LEED-certified facilities, and sources sustainably from indigenous communities worldwide.

labor.s: Owned by Estée Lauder, which has published supply chain codes addressing labor standards including forced labor prohibition and child labor bans. However, code lacks comprehensive coverage of collective bargaining rights, living wages, and grievance mechanisms.

dei.s: Aveda brand emphasizes diversity through product development and community partnerships with indigenous peoples globally. Parent company Estée Lauder maintains DEI initiatives, though facing broader industry headwinds regarding DEI program scrutiny.

animals.s: Aveda has been cruelty-free since founding in 1978 with Leaping Bunny certification as of 2022. Became 100% vegan as of 2020, eliminating all animal-derived ingredients and by-products from formulas.

guns.s: Not applicable; Aveda is a beauty and personal care company with no firearms operations or sales.

privacy.s: Aveda's privacy practices reflect parent company Estée Lauder standards. Limited public information available regarding comprehensive data privacy policies or third-party audits of data security practices.

execPay.s: Not disclosed. Aveda operates as semi-autonomous subsidiary of Estée Lauder Companies. Executive compensation data not publicly available for subsidiary operations.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
